intent: Implement Launchdeck v1 as a cross-platform local CLI lifecycle control tool with explicit config, managed runtime state, safe cleanup, and stable automation contracts.
blocked_reason: Native subagent tools are available, but this environment requires the user to explicitly ask for subagents before spawning them; planning was completed inline.
allowed_optimization_scope: Refactor current CLI/runtime/config code only when behavior remains compatible with the contracts.
Add internal adapter modules for platform-owned path, shell, process, executable lookup, cleanup, and log behavior.
Add tests, examples, and documentation needed to prove v1 behavior.
acceptance_obligations: Every v1 command that supports JSON emits parseable success and classified failure envelopes.
Foreground execution preserves child exit codes.
Managed execution records state and logs and prevents duplicate starts.
Runtime inspection preserves stale records and logs.
Clean is dry-run by default and refuses root, out-of-root, empty, and ambiguous targets.
Error codes are stable and documented.
Verification claims identify dev-ready, platform-ready, or cross-platform-ready evidence level.

consequence_gate.coverage: CA-001 through CA-022 are represented in plan.md and this contract.
stop_and_reopen_conditions: A required CA obligation cannot be implemented without changing v1 scope.
Cleanup safety cannot be proven for root/out-of-root/ambiguous targets.
Managed process stop cannot be made inspectable and failure-aware.
Cross-platform-ready is requested without all-OS smoke evidence.
A deferred integration becomes a required v1 surface.

consequence_obligations.design: Clean uses dry-run, safe, all-confirmed, refused, and skipped states; destructive cleanup is never implicit.
Managed processes use running, stopped, stale, unknown, and stop_failed states with duplicate-start refusal.
JSON uses shared success, error, and partial envelopes.
Config protocol supports no-config, configured, invalid, and unsupported-version states.
Runtime state and logs are durable recovery evidence.
consequence_obligations.validation: Invalid state, stale record, and missing-log tests.

consequence_obligations.design: Release support is tracked across Windows, macOS, and Linux.
Runtime adapters own platform-specific execution behavior.
Config schema remains small, explicit, readable, and v1-compatible.
Error codes are stable compatibility fields.
First-run flow orders inspection before mutation.
Doctor is read-only with ok, warn, and error findings.
Tasks is read-only inventory.
Execution respects longRunning, foreground child exits, duplicate managed starts, and restart partial states.
Runtime commands preserve records/logs and handle missing-log and stop-failed states.
Clean defaults to dry-run, splits safe/risky, and refuses unsafe paths.
Platform-sensitive path, shell, process, clean, and log behavior is adapter-owned.
Verification claims are gated by evidence level.
Init handles no-config, existing-config, and ancestor-config without silent overwrite.
Project-root behavior is deterministic and visible in JSON.
JSON and exit codes distinguish success, errors, child exits, and partial failures.
Classified errors are preferred; internal_error is rare and review-worthy.
v1 boundary remains local single-project CLI and excludes deferred integrations.
